title: External-validation sequencing — how open standards and platform projects gate scope widening on independent implementation/operational experience (prep for the pilot-before-polyglot decision)
summary: Prep research for decision #2089 (filed by the 2026-07-01 external consultant review, which found the sequencing drifted: polyglot generation #463/#507 ratified AND built while zero external users exist and the dogfooding claim was not yet true; we:reports/2026-07-01-program-external-consultant-review.md). TREE GROUND TRUTH (2026-07-02 — two review premises moved the same day it ran): dogfood is now real-but-partial (SSR keystone #2016 resolved with live code at we:.eleventy.js:280 + we:scripts/lib/component-render-build-hook.cjs + fui:blocks/renderers/component-render/buildHarness.ts; chrome migrated #865; home grid SSR #2019; still open: #2018/#2020/#2021/#866/#867, epic #777 open) — truthful for chrome + one SSR surface, false as a blanket claim. Parity keystone #2017 shipped (fui:plugs/webtheme/manifestLoader.ts) but zero parity targets measured (#1243 superseded-as-stub by open #2022; #2022-#2025 all open). The gated deploy #1137 is technology-ready and humanGate'd (we:functions/_middleware.js splash gate built; residual = credentialed wrangler deploy). The polyglot MECHANISM is already built through its first increment (#507 resolved 2026-06-15: IR emit engine #547, first .NET target #548/#661, conformance gate #549 via #506, regression corpus #551; statute we:docs/agent/platform-decisions.md#forward-generation-adapters) — so a gate can only govern the UN-BUILT widening (new language targets / new emit forms; #1735 itself sits under its own ratified empirical trigger); it cannot retroact. No pilot-adopter STORY exists in the backlog, though the resolved adopter deck #1214/#1360 already ships the flagged claim. PRIOR ART — the uniform cross-process pattern: an internal test suite is necessary but never sufficient; graduation/widening requires INDEPENDENT implementation or operational experience, and the gate is always STRUCTURAL (a stage machine), never advisory. TC39: Stage 4 requires >=2 compatible shipped implementations + test262 + practical experience (WE shipped the test262 analogue — the #506 golden vectors — with zero implementation-experience analogue: the exact inversion Stage 4 forbids). W3C/CSS WG: CR exit = adequate implementation experience, customarily two independent interoperable implementations. IETF: running code + RFC 6410 operational experience for Internet Standard. Baseline (WebDX): status is an evidence state derived from shipped availability, never a roadmap claim (the internal analogue: we:docs/agent/platform-decisions.md#first-party-dogfood — claims must trail the tree). semver 0.x: production usage is the graduation trigger. Design-system pilot practice (EightShapes/Curtis; Polaris/Lightning): 1-3 real pilot teams, representative scope, timed window, defined adoption+feedback criteria; dogfood-before-external is the field norm, but the dogfood gates the PUBLIC CLAIM, not all internal progress. Kubernetes feature gates: beta->GA requires real-world usage. Rust: stabilization on experience reports — the un-gate trigger is a named evidence artifact, not a date. SYNTHESIS -> forks (post-skeptic form; the prep skeptic landed five attacks, folded in): Fork 1 (validation order) staged evidence ladder encoded as blockedBy edges — the gated deploy's bar is CLAIM-INDEXED not inventory-indexed (met once a named claims-truth audit passes, sweeping the site AND the resolved adopter deck #1214/#1360 which still carries the flagged 'we eat our own cooking' claim); pilot recruitment blockedBy the live deploy; the UNGATED public stage blockedBy the full dogfood ratchet #867 + an audit re-run — vs strict-serial full-dogfood-first (a stricter threshold on the same audience-indexed truthfulness axis, protecting an audience the splash gate already restricts) vs pilot-first (burns the one high-cost external asset on an undeployed artifact). Fork 2 (polyglot gate) HARD prospective PER-TARGET gate — codified rule: every new target's start cites CURRENT external-adopter evidence (the start-gate sibling of #463 fork (c)'s release-gate mechanic), BOOTSTRAPPED by a blockedBy edge to the pilot-evidence item, never permanently satisfied by the first retro; scope is a predicate (adds a new generation target/emit form), not an item list — maintenance of shipped artifacts and the #912 workbench family stay ungated, and #1735 is EXEMPT (already governed by the ratified forward-emit-dedicated-ir empirical maturityTrigger, 'not a backlog edge' — the two gates compose) — vs advisory note (an invariant a selector cannot see; the filing-layer judgment residual stated honestly, tag-keyed check:standards rule filed as follow-up) vs no gate (the state the June drift actually happened under). Pilot success criteria (the gate's concrete trigger): one external team, production-bound surface, >=3 FUI blocks + >=2 WE intents PLUS >=1 forward-generated artifact consumed (gen-wrapper wrapper or MaaS-served module — without that leg the retro produces no evidence about the forward-generation contract the gate turns on), 6-week window, >=5 filed issues or a written gap report, written retro with continue/churn verdict; the retro artifact is the un-gate trigger, and a failed pilot re-opens the decision rather than silently un-gating.
